heroes.note: Foreign outflows accelerating, rupiah at record lows, fiscal concerns trigger rating downgrade worries, MSCI-related selling pressure.
Market worst globally YTD (-34%), foreign investor exodus broadening, geopolitical risks sustaining capital flight through mid-2026.

news.title: Indonesia stocks tumble to worst year globally; forex reserves fall to 2-yr low amid rupiah crisis
news.impact: Global confidence collapse deepening; $2B BI intervention in April signals mounting external pressure; rating agencies monitoring fiscal resilience through mid-2026.

news.title: BI Rate hiked to 5.25% in May to defend rupiah; inflation concerns linger despite 3.08% May CPI rise
news.impact: 50bp surprise hike aims to attract portfolio inflows and stabilize currency; rupiah pressure expected to persist through June before seasonal improvement in July–Aug.

news.title: Parliament passes sweeping commodities legislation; government tightens grip on mining sector management
news.impact: Increased state intervention fears; potential export controls and pricing curbs may deter foreign investment and weigh on sector valuations through 2H 2026.

news.title: FTSE Russell removes eight Indonesian stocks from indices; MSCI downgrade speculation grows
news.impact: Passive fund outflows accelerating; potential MSCI downgrade would trigger cascading foreign sales estimated at USD 5–8B; investor sentiment near cyclical lows.

news.title: Trade surplus collapses to $0.09B in April; oil import surge underscores external vulnerability
news.impact: Fiscal and balance-of-payments risks intensifying; narrowing surplus with soaring energy costs signals weaker external resilience; concerns over further rupiah depreciation near-term.

news.title: May inflation at 3.08% YoY highest in West Papua; food price pressures resurface amid seasonal demand
news.impact: Inflation creeping above target midpoint; imported inflation via weaker rupiah poses risk to 2.5% ±1% target through 2H 2026; BI likely to hold or hike further if energy prices remain elevated.

regulations.title: BI FX Purchase Threshold Lowered
regulations.sub: USD 25,000 per person/month limit · Effective June 2026

precedents.html: In 2013, similar rupiah depreciation pressures and capital outflows triggered a 16% currency decline over 6 months; BI intervention and eventual policy tightening arrested declines by end-2013.
The 2018 emerging-market selloff saw Indonesia's market fall ~22% YTD before stabilizing; rupiah then recovered 8–10% once Fed signaled rate-cut pause and BI tightened policy.
1997–98 Asian Financial Crisis saw Indonesian equities collapse >70% and rupiah plunge 78%; recovery took 18+ months and required IMF support, structural reforms, and global liquidity relief.

geo.html: Middle East conflict → elevated oil prices → energy import bill surge → trade deficit narrowing → external reserve pressure. Now: Brent near $97/bbl; May trade surplus collapsed to $0.09B vs. historical avg. of $1.5–2B.

US monetary tightness + Fed rate expectations → USD strengthening → emerging-market capital flight → Indonesia down -34% YTD (worst among 90+ global indices); foreign sell-offs mount. Now: DXY near 99.4; BI forex reserves hit 2-yr low of $146.2B in April.

China growth moderation + global trade slowdown → weaker commodity demand → nickel & coal volatility → IDX mining sector down -8.5% Thu; manufacturing PMI resilient at 50 but export orders soft. Now: Coal up +3.5% on China supply shock; Indonesia export growth still lagging.
